About Victorian Plumbing Group
Victorian Plumbing is the UK's leading bathroom retailer, offering an unrivalled high-quality product range and excellent stock availability to B2C and trade customers.
Victorian Plumbing offers its customers a one-stop shop solution for the entire bathroom with own and third party brands across a wide spectrum of price points. Victorian Plumbing product design and supply chain strengths are complemented by its creative and brand-focused marketing strategy to drive significant and growing traffic to its platforms.
Launched in 2025, the Group also includes MFI, an online-only UK retailer, offering a growing range of stylish homewares and furniture.
Headquartered in the North West, Victorian Plumbing and MFI employ staff across several locations in the UK.

latest.title: CEO Succession
latest.content:
25 Feb 2026 07:00
RNS Number : 2487U
Victorian Plumbing Group PLC
25 February 2026
THIS ANNOUNCEMENT CONTAINS INSIDE INFORMATION
25 February 2026
Victorian Plumbing Group plc
("Victorian Plumbing" or the "Group")
CEO Succession
Victorian Plumbing (AIM: VIC)
, the UK's leading bathroom retailer,
today announces that Mark Radcliffe, the Group's Founder and current Chief Executive Officer ("CEO") has decided he will relinquish his role as CEO with effect from 31 March 2026 but will remain on the Board as a non-executive Founder Director.
Following a thorough review of succession options, the Nomination Committee concluded that Stephnie Judge, currently the Group's Managing Director ("Group MD"), was the natural choice to succeed Mark. The Board is therefore pleased to confirm that Stephnie has been appointed to the Board to succeed Mark as CEO with effect from 1 April 2026. Mark and Stephnie will continue to work closely together to ensure a smooth transition into their new roles.
Stephnie joined Victorian Plumbing in 2013 and, following a number of promotions, was appointed Group MD in 2020. She has worked closely with Mark on all operational and commercial aspects of the business and has played a key role in the successful development and execution of the Group's strategy. Her leadership has been instrumental in driving the Group's growth and delivering key strategic initiatives, including: the recent warehouse transformation, the acquisitions of Victoria Plum and Sovereign Transport Services, numerous technology and customer proposition improvements, and the Group's entry into the homewares market through the reimagined MFI brand. The Board is confident that her extensive operational experience and intimate knowledge of the business mean Stephnie is ideally placed to lead the Group.
Mark founded Victorian Plumbing in 2000 and has grown the business into the UK's leading bathroom retailer. As a non-executive Founder Director, Mark will remain actively engaged with the Group and its strategic direction, and will continue to work closely with Stephnie and the wider team. Mark also remains a substantial and committed shareholder in Victorian Plumbing.
In conjunction with the CEO succession plan, the Remuneration Committee of the Board is currently reviewing the Executive Directors' reward and the long term incentivisation of Victorian Plumbing's executive leadership team with a view to ensuring that it remains optimised. The Board will provide an update to shareholders in due course.
Philip Bowcock, Chair of Victorian Plumbing Group plc, commented:
"I am delighted that Stephnie has agreed to step up as CEO of Victorian Plumbing. Her diligent approach to the delivery of the Group's strategic initiatives stands us in very good stead as we look to the next phase of growth. I have worked closely with Stephnie since becoming Chair and I share Mark's confidence in her excellent leadership credentials. I have no doubt that she is the right person to lead Victorian Plumbing through the next chapter and beyond.
"The Board and the Group will continue to benefit from Mark's deep knowledge of the company he has built, the marketing and technology that drives it and the wider retail environment in his Founder Director role. I look forward to working with him and Stephnie in their new roles."
Mark Radcliffe, CEO, commented:
"Victorian Plumbing has been on an amazing journey in the 25 years since I founded it and I feel that now is the right time to hand over the reins. Stephnie is the right person to guide Victorian Plumbing through the next phase of growth and take advantage of the significant opportunities that lie ahead.
"I have every confidence that Victorian Plumbing will continue to go from strength to strength and I look forward to my new Founder Director role."
Stephnie Judge, CEO designate, said:
"I am honoured and excited to take over from Mark as CEO of Victorian Plumbing, a company that he started from his parents' garden shed over two and a half decades ago. I am confident that the strategy we have put in place together is the right one, and I look forward to working with the executive leadership team and Mark in his new Founder Director role as we continue to win market share while delivering excellent value and choice for our customers."
The information communicated within this announcement is deemed to constitute inside information under the Market Abuse Regulation (EU) No. 596/2014 (as amended) which is part of UK law by virtue of the European Union (Withdrawal) Act 2018. The person responsible for arranging the release of this announcement on behalf of the Company is Alex Singer, Company Secretary.
-ENDS-

latest.title: AGM Trading Update, Acquisition & Results Notice
latest.content:
25 Feb 2026 07:00
RNS Number : 2482U
Victorian Plumbing Group PLC
25 February 2026
25 February 2026
Victorian Plumbing Group plc
("Victorian Plumbing" or the "Group")
AGM Trading Update, Acquisition and Notice of Results
Victorian Plumbing (AIM: VIC), the UK's leading bathroom retailer, today provides an update on trading for the first 21 weeks of its financial year ending 30 September 2026 ("FY26"), in advance of its Annual General Meeting which is being held today at 9:30am GMT.
The Group is also announcing the bolt on acquisition of a transportation services business and providing notice of its interim results.
AGM Trading Update
The Board is pleased to report that the positive momentum experienced in Q1, as highlighted in the announcement of the Group's FY25 Results on 3 December 2025, has continued into Q2. Revenue for the first 21 weeks of FY26 increased by 9%, driven by order volume growth across all categories with margins maintained at historic levels.
The Board is confident in delivering FY26 revenue and adjusted PBT in line with current market expectations1.
Acquisition, Listing Application and Total Voting Rights
On 31 January 2026, Victorian Plumbing acquired the entire issued share capital of Sovereign Transport Services Limited and Sovereign Commercials Limited (together "Sovereign"). Sovereign is a transportation services business based in Merseyside, providing haulage services to the Group and to a small number of other goods-based businesses in the North of England. The acquisition provides the Group with increased control over customer fulfilment and an opportunity to improve other areas of transportation over the medium term.
The purchase price (net of cash acquired) of £3.4m is subject to adjustments for normalised working capital and represents c.5x Sovereign's estimated annual adjusted EBITDA. The acquisition has been funded almost entirely from the Group's existing cash reserves, with 65,000 new ordinary shares of £0.001 each in the Company ("Consideration Shares") to be allotted and issued to the seller in part satisfaction of the purchase price payable. Acquisition related fees are expected to be c.£0.4m.
A
n application has been made to the London Stock Exchange for the Consideration Shares to be admitted to trading on AIM ("Admission"). Admission is expected to become effective and trading is expected to commence in the Consideration Shares at 8.00am on 27 February 2026. When issued, the Consideration Shares will rank pari passu with the existing ordinary shares of £0.001 each in the Company ("Ordinary Shares").
Following Admission, the Company's issued share capital will comprise 328,769,068 Ordinary Shares, each carrying the right to one vote. This figure of 328,769,068 may therefore be used by shareholders as the denominator for calculations by which they will determine whether they are required to notify their interest in, or a change to their interest in, the Company under the FCA's Disclosure Guidance and Transparency Rules.
Notice of Results
The Group will report its interim results for the six months ending 31 March 2026 on 19 May 2026.
1
Victorian Plumbing believes that current consensus market expectations for FY26 are for revenues of £329.5m and adjusted PBT of £21.9m.
-ENDS-

latest.title: Posting of Annual Report and Notice of AGM
latest.content:
18 Dec 2025 07:00
RNS Number : 0048M
Victorian Plumbing Group PLC
18 December 2025
18 December 2025
Victorian Plumbing Group plc
("Victorian Plumbing" or the "Company")
Posting of Annual Report and Accounts and Notice of Annual General Meeting
Following the release of its full year results for the year ended 30 September 2025 ("FY25") on 3 December 2025, Victorian Plumbing (AIM: VIC), the UK's leading bathroom retailer, has today posted its Annual Report and Accounts for FY25 ("Annual Report") to shareholders.
The Company also announces that its 2026 Annual General Meeting ("AGM") will be held at 9:30am on 25 February 2026 at the Company's head office, 1 Sustainability Way, Farington Moss, Leyland, PR26 6TB.
Shareholder participation and engagement is important to Victorian Plumbing. The Company strongly encourages voting by proxy and sending any questions in advance to
ir@victorianplumbing.co.uk
.
Copies of the Annual Report and Notice of AGM will shortly be available to view on the Company's website at
www.victorianplumbingplc.com/investors/key-documents
.
-ENDS-

company_30s.what_they_do: Victorian Plumbing supplies and installs plumbing fixtures, fittings and related services across the UK, targeting both residential and commercial customers. They also offer maintenance contracts and a growing online ordering platform.
company_30s.why_they_matter: The UK plumbing market is expanding because of stricter water‑efficiency regulations and a wave of home‑renovation projects. Victorian Plumbing has a nationwide network and a strong brand that lets it capture a sizeable share of that demand.
company_30s.current_state: The company has just completed a CEO transition, announced a bolt‑on acquisition, and reported a 9% rise in revenue for the first 21 weeks of FY26E. Cash flow is stable, and the business is moving from a recovery phase into a growth phase, but it remains trading far below its pre‑crash valuation.

rns_narrative: The AGM revealed a fresh management lineup, a strategic acquisition to broaden the product mix, and a 9% revenue lift that signals operational stability.
The company has shifted its focus to higher‑margin services and a digital sales channel, which could generate new recurring revenue.
Investor communications emphasize that the recent price plunge was a market overreaction rather than a business failure.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Quiet optimism after a leadership overhaul
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: Bulls point to the CEO change, the acquisition, and the 9% revenue growth as signs that the company is now on a sustainable trajectory.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Bears worry that the valuation remains too high for the current earnings base and that the market may still punish the stock if growth stalls.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: The chatter has moved from neutral to mildly bullish as investors react to the AGM updates and the uptick in revenue.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends is flat, meaning the market is largely unaware of the upcoming catalysts—maximum surprise potential.

near_term_catalysts: The next quarterly earnings report could confirm the 9% revenue lift and provide a surprise in operating margin.
Completion of the bolt‑on acquisition should lift the product portfolio and open new sales channels, potentially driving a price spike.
Any announcement of a new digital platform or partnership with a major retailer would create a volume surge and a breakout.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for a break above the recent 52‑week low with a volume spike of at least 30% above the 20‑day average. A clear reversal of the downtrend on the daily chart will also signal that mean reversion is underway.
expect_next.timing_regime: The stock appears to be in the early phase of a recovery after a deep crash. APEX compression suggests a tight spring, so a single catalyst could unleash a significant bounce.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: The RED signal indicates an average 162.8% return when the price rebounds from a 70–95% drop. With recent revenue growth and a fresh CEO, the likelihood of a strong bounce is high. If the price recovers to 50% of its pre‑crash level, that’s roughly a 100% upside, and the historical average suggests even larger moves are possible.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: The main risk is that the valuation remains too high for the current earnings base; a modest earnings miss or a regulatory setback could push the price back down. The worst‑case downside is a return to the current low, but the stock could also be wiped out if the company fails to deliver the projected growth.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential upside is roughly 100%–200% versus a downside that is capped at the current price level. The risk/reward ratio is therefore in the ballpark of 1:2 to 1:3, making this an attractive asymmetric play.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter after a clear breakout above the 52‑week low with volume >30% above the 20‑day average. Size the position to 10% of the portfolio, and set a stop just below the recent support level. Use a trailing stop to lock in gains as the price climbs.
playbook.if_watching: Wait for the earnings release or the completion of the acquisition. If the stock closes above the 50‑day moving average on that day, that is a strong confirmation to go long.
playbook.if_passing: No genuine terminal risk has surfaced; therefore the default stance remains bullish until clear evidence of delisting, bankruptcy, or fraud emerges.

ai_insights.bottom_line: A deep crash, a quiet market, and a CEO‑led turnaround suggest a high‑reward contrarian play—expect a significant bounce if the company delivers on its 9% revenue lift and acquisition goals.
